rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 11, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT URBAN BAY PROPERTIES, INC., DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to Fourth Cause of Action (common count for money had and received) and fifth cause of action (breach of fiduciary duty). Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to eighth and tenth causes of action (B&P Code section 17200 and constructive trust), no opposition. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to ninth cause of action (unjust enrichment) which is not a cause of action but rather a principle underlying certain doctrines and rememdies. See Jogani vs. Superior Court (2008) 165 Cal.App. 4th 901, 911. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs' Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 11, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT URBAN BAY PROPERTIES, INC., Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend to specify which defendants they are seeking fees from. Motion granted with ten days leave to amend as to punitive damages for plaintiffs to plead facts amounting to fraudulent, oppressive or malicious conduct. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JULIA ELLIS VS. HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 11, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT BARRY GIVAN Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Motion granted. Givan is not subject to general jurisdiction because he lacks substantial, systematic, and continuous contact with California. Givan is not subject to specific jurisdiction. He did not purposefully avail himself of the benefits and privileges of conducting business in California, was employed in Nevada, and served customers who come to him of their own accord. There is not substantial nexus between Givan's minimal contacts with California and the alleged incident. It would be unfair and unreasonable to exercise jurisdiction over Givvan in California. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JULIA ELLIS VS. HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 11, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC., HARRAH'S OPERATING COMPANY, INC., HARVEYS TAHOE MANAGEMENT COMPANY, INC., and, HARVEY'S LAKE TAHOE Motion to Quash for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction. Motion denied. Although there does not appear to be general jurisdiction over these defendants, they are subject to specific jurisdiction in California. Defendants purposefully availed themselves of the benefits and privileges of conducting business in California through their various methods of advertising correspondence and interactive website. A substantial connection exists between plaintiff's claim and defendants' activities that enticed plaintiff to visit Harvey's where she was injured. The exercise of jurisdiction over these defendants comports with traditional motions of fair play and substantial justice and is reasonable. Given the depth of solicitation of California patronage undertaken by these defendants, it is reasonable that they would foresee that they would be subjected to California jurisdiction. See Snowney vs. Harrah's Entertainment, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1054. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEFANIE NAIFEH VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Stefanie Naifeh'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 10/14/09)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 11, 2010, line 7, Plaintiff Stefanie Naifeh's Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 10/14/09). Motion denied, no reasonable probability that plaintiff will prevail on the merits of her claim. Plaintiff presents no evidence or authority that overcomnes section 2.5 of the Purchase Agreement which limits JP Morgan's liability for borrower claims made on a loan executed by Washington Mutual. CC section 2923.6 is a statement of legislative intent, and no "violation" of CC section 2923.5 is shown. No evidence supports a finding that defendants are debt collectors under the RFDCPA, or that a claim under that act would apply to this foreclosure situation. No evidence supports any actionable fraud or misrepresentations by these defendants, and no specific intent to defraud or injure plaintiff demonstrated. No claim stated for declaratory relief. No evidence presented that these defendants engaged in unlawful business practices under the UCL. =(302/CWW)
